verses.text: The battle goes against Israel on Mount Gilboa; the men flee and fall, and Saul's sons — including faithful Jonathan — are slain. The archers close in and wound the king. The doom Samuel foretold arrives, sweeping away the godly with the guilty in one dark day.

The battle goes against Israel on Mount Gilboa; the men flee and fall, and Saul's sons — including faithful Jonathan — are slain. The archers close in and wound the king. The doom Samuel foretold arrives, sweeping away the godly with the guilty in one dark day.

The battle goes against Israel on Mount Gilboa; the men flee and fall, and Saul's sons — including faithful Jonathan — are slain. The archers close in and wound the king. The doom Samuel foretold arrives, sweeping away the godly with the guilty in one dark day.

Wounded and dreading capture, Saul falls on his own sword, and his armourbearer dies beside him. So Saul, his sons, and his men perish together. The reign that began in promise ends in suicide and ruin — the bitter harvest of a life that turned from God.

Wounded and dreading capture, Saul falls on his own sword, and his armourbearer dies beside him. So Saul, his sons, and his men perish together. The reign that began in promise ends in suicide and ruin — the bitter harvest of a life that turned from God.

Wounded and dreading capture, Saul falls on his own sword, and his armourbearer dies beside him. So Saul, his sons, and his men perish together. The reign that began in promise ends in suicide and ruin — the bitter harvest of a life that turned from God.

Seeing the king dead and the army broken, the Israelites abandon their cities and flee, and the Philistines move in. One man's long rebellion costs a whole people their homes. The sin of a leader can bring loss far beyond himself, scattering those he was meant to protect.

The Philistines strip Saul's body, cut off his head, and fasten his corpse to the wall of Beth-shan, parading his armour before their idols. The king who would not honour God is dishonoured by His enemies. How far the once-tall, chosen king has fallen at the end.

The Philistines strip Saul's body, cut off his head, and fasten his corpse to the wall of Beth-shan, parading his armour before their idols. The king who would not honour God is dishonoured by His enemies. How far the once-tall, chosen king has fallen at the end.

The Philistines strip Saul's body, cut off his head, and fasten his corpse to the wall of Beth-shan, parading his armour before their idols. The king who would not honour God is dishonoured by His enemies. How far the once-tall, chosen king has fallen at the end.

But the men of Jabesh-gilead, whom Saul once rescued, travel by night to take down the bodies, burn and bury them, and fast seven days. In the darkest hour, gratitude shines: they repay an old kindness with brave devotion. Even a tragic life can leave a debt of love behind.
